title: Cohort Definitions
description: Cohort Definitions allow researchers to specify inclusion and exclusion criteria that identify a population of interest from the OMOP CDM. Cohorts form the foundational building blocks for analyses, prediction models, incidence rate calculations, and patient-level investigations across the platform.
tips: Use concept sets to capture all clinically relevant codes for a condition or drug, then reference them across multiple cohort definitions to maintain consistency.
Apply index date logic carefully—cohort start date selection significantly affects downstream analysis results.
Validate cohort counts against expected prevalence estimates before using a cohort in a formal analysis.
Version your cohort definitions and document changes to support reproducibility and peer review.
Use the cohort characterization feature to quickly profile a newly defined cohort and identify unexpected populations.
